france.name: Atlantic influence
france.description: Atlantic weather moderates western France, bringing cooling influence, humidity, and vintage variation.

france.name: Mediterranean influence
france.description: The Mediterranean helps drive warmer, sunnier southern French growing conditions.

germany.name: Rhine corridor
germany.description: Major river corridors help reflect light and soften cool continental conditions in German vineyards.

austria.name: Danube River
austria.description: The Danube moderates temperature, reflects light, and shapes many of Austria's best vineyard sites.

greece.name: Aegean maritime influence
greece.description: The Aegean brings cooling winds and maritime moderation to many Greek vineyards.

italy.name: Alpine barrier
italy.description: The Alps provide shelter while altitude and valley exposures strongly shape northern Italian climates.

spain.name: Atlantic influence
spain.description: Atlantic conditions cool and moisten Spain's northwest and parts of the north.

spain.name: Mediterranean influence
spain.description: Mediterranean warmth raises temperatures and sunshine on Spain's eastern and southeastern side.

portugal.name: Atlantic influence
portugal.description: The Atlantic cools Portugal's western vineyards and increases humidity and rainfall near the coast.

usa.name: Pacific influence
usa.description: Cold Pacific influence, currents, and fog are central moderating factors for many West Coast vineyards.

chile.name: Humboldt Current
chile.description: The cold Humboldt Current cools Chile's coast, preserving freshness and slowing ripening in coastal zones.

chile.name: Andes influence
chile.description: The Andes create altitude, cool nights, and a rain-shadow effect that shape Chilean viticulture.

argentina.name: Andes influence
argentina.description: The Andes provide altitude, meltwater for irrigation, and strong day-night variation in Argentina's vineyards.

south-africa.name: Benguela Current
south-africa.description: The cold Benguela Current flows north up the west coast, cooling Cape vineyards and helping preserve acidity.

australia.name: Southern Ocean influence
australia.description: Southern Ocean air helps moderate many Australian regions, especially cooler coastal and island sites.

new-zealand.name: Tasman Sea influence
new-zealand.description: The Tasman Sea moderates western New Zealand and contributes to a cool maritime growing environment.

new-zealand.name: Pacific influence
new-zealand.description: The Pacific Ocean brings maritime moderation, wind, and freshness to many eastern New Zealand sites.

bordeaux.name: Atlantic influence
bordeaux.description: Atlantic weather cools Bordeaux, increases rainfall, and makes vintage variation important.

bordeaux.name: Gironde estuary
bordeaux.description: The Gironde, Garonne, and Dordogne moderate local temperatures and shape humidity patterns.

muscadet.name: Atlantic influence
muscadet.description: Strong Atlantic influence keeps Muscadet cool and supports high-acid, light-bodied wines.

anjou-saumur.name: Loire River
anjou-saumur.description: The Loire helps moderate temperature and supports the region's varied sparkling, white, and rosé styles.

touraine.name: Loire River
touraine.description: The Loire corridor adds moderation while inland influence increases toward the east.

central-vineyards.name: Loire River
central-vineyards.description: The Loire and its tributaries help moderate this otherwise cool inland part of the valley.

northern-rhone.name: Rhone River
northern-rhone.description: The Rhone corridor shapes vineyard exposure and local mesoclimate in the steep northern valley.

northern-rhone.name: Mistral
northern-rhone.description: The Mistral dries vineyards, reduces disease pressure, and can lower temperatures.

southern-rhone.name: Rhone River
southern-rhone.description: The Rhone valley forms a major corridor through the warm southern part of the region.

southern-rhone.name: Mistral
southern-rhone.description: The Mistral helps dry vineyards and can moderate the heat in Southern Rhone vineyards.

alsace.name: Vosges rain shadow
alsace.description: The Vosges reduce rainfall and help make Alsace notably drier and sunnier than many nearby regions.

champagne.name: Marne valley corridor
champagne.description: The Marne valley shapes exposure and contributes to local mesoclimate differences in Champagne.

provence.name: Mediterranean Sea
provence.description: Mediterranean influence brings warmth, sunlight, and sea moderation to Provence.

languedoc-roussillon.name: Mediterranean Sea
languedoc-roussillon.description: Mediterranean warmth is moderated in places by coastal exposure, wind, and altitude.

mosel.name: Mosel River
mosel.description: The Mosel reflects light and moderates temperature in this very cool, steep river valley.

rheingau.name: Rhine River
rheingau.description: The Rhine reflects light and helps moderate temperatures for Rheingau vineyards.

nahe.name: Nahe River
nahe.description: River valleys and mixed exposures soften the climate across Nahe.

rheinhessen.name: Rhine influence
rheinhessen.description: The Rhine helps moderate temperature while sheltered sites add ripening potential.

pfalz.name: Haardt Mountains
pfalz.description: Mountain shelter reduces rainfall and helps make Pfalz warmer and drier than many German regions.

franken.name: Main River
franken.description: The Main river corridor and slope exposure help this inland region ripen fruit.

niederosterreich.name: Danube River
niederosterreich.description: The Danube moderates temperature and reflects light across Lower Austria.

burgenland.name: Lake Neusiedl
burgenland.description: Lake Neusiedl moderates temperature and promotes humidity and botrytis potential.

steiermark.name: Alpine cooling
steiermark.description: Altitude and Alpine influence help preserve freshness in Steiermark.

wachau.name: Danube River
wachau.description: The Danube is a central moderating factor in the Wachau, alongside stone terraces and diurnal range.

tokaj.name: Tisza and Bodrog rivers
tokaj.description: River mists encourage botrytis and are central to Tokaj's sweet wine production.

eger.name: Higher inland sites
eger.description: Altitude and inland continental conditions help keep Eger cooler than warmer southern Hungarian areas.

santorini.name: Aegean winds
santorini.description: Strong Aegean winds cool the island and explain the traditional basket-trained vines.

naoussa.name: Mountain influence
naoussa.description: Altitude and mountain influence help keep Naoussa cooler than many Mediterranean regions.

amyndeon.name: Altitude and lake influence
amyndeon.description: Altitude and nearby lakes help make Amyndeon one of Greece's cooler wine regions.

alto-adige-trentino-friuli.name: Alpine influence
alto-adige-trentino-friuli.description: Altitude, Alpine shelter, and valley exposures strongly shape this cool northern area.

veneto.name: Lake Garda
veneto.description: Lake Garda helps moderate nearby vineyards and supports fresher styles in parts of Veneto.

veneto.name: Alpine protection
veneto.description: The Alps shelter parts of Veneto and create useful site variation.

tuscany.name: Tyrrhenian influence
tuscany.description: Sea influence and inland hills help moderate temperature across Tuscany.

marche.name: Adriatic influence
marche.description: The Adriatic helps moderate Marche, especially for white-wine areas near the coast.

lazio.name: Tyrrhenian influence
lazio.description: Coastal influence and elevated volcanic sites help moderate Lazio.

campania.name: Tyrrhenian influence
campania.description: Sea influence and altitude are important moderating factors in Campania.

puglia.name: Adriatic influence
puglia.description: Sea influence helps moderate heat in parts of Puglia despite the region's warm, sunny climate.

sicily.name: Sea breezes
sicily.description: Sea breezes help moderate Sicily, while altitude is critical around Etna.

piemonte.name: Alpine shelter
piemonte.description: Alpine shelter, hills, and aspect are important ripening factors in Piemonte.

rias-baixas.name: Atlantic influence
rias-baixas.description: Strong Atlantic influence keeps Rias Baixas cool, wet, and well ventilated.

rioja.name: Ebro corridor
rioja.description: The Ebro corridor and altitude differences help create Rioja's subregional variation.

rioja.name: Mountain shelter
rioja.description: Mountain shelter helps protect Rioja from harsher weather while Atlantic influence remains important in cooler zones.

ribera-del-duero.name: Duero River
ribera-del-duero.description: The Duero corridor and high elevation produce a marked diurnal range in Ribera del Duero.

jerez.name: Atlantic influence
jerez.description: Atlantic breezes moderate Jerez and are important for flor and Sherry ageing conditions.

penedes.name: Mediterranean influence
penedes.description: Mediterranean warmth is moderated by altitude in higher Penedes sites.

navarra.name: North-south transition
navarra.description: Navarra bridges cooler Atlantic influence in the north and warmer Mediterranean conditions in the south.

jumilla.name: Altitude moderation
jumilla.description: Altitude helps moderate this otherwise hot, dry inland region.

valencia.name: Mediterranean influence
valencia.description: Coastal influence moderates parts of Valencia, while inland areas are warmer.

vinho-verde.name: Atlantic influence
vinho-verde.description: Atlantic influence keeps Vinho Verde cool, wet, and relatively low in alcohol.

douro.name: Douro River
douro.description: The Douro river corridor and terraced slopes create major mesoclimate differences across the valley.

dao.name: Mountain protection
dao.description: Surrounding mountains shelter Dao and help shape a more moderate inland climate.

california.name: Pacific cooling
california.description: Cold Pacific influence and fog are core moderating factors in California viticulture.

napa-valley.name: San Pablo Bay
napa-valley.description: Cool air and fog from San Pablo Bay moderate the south of Napa and influence valley mesoclimates.

sonoma.name: Pacific fog
sonoma.description: Pacific fog and cool-air corridors are key moderating factors across Sonoma.

central-coast.name: Pacific influence
central-coast.description: Ocean air, fog, and gaps in the coastal ranges strongly moderate the Central Coast.

oregon.name: Pacific influence
oregon.description: Pacific influence helps keep Oregon relatively cool with a long growing season.

washington-state.name: Cascade rain shadow
washington-state.description: The Cascades create a rain shadow, giving eastern Washington warm, dry conditions with cool nights.

aconcagua.name: Pacific cooling
aconcagua.description: Pacific breezes and the cold Humboldt Current moderate the western side of Aconcagua.

central-valley.name: Andes cooling
central-valley.description: Andes influence helps cool nights and maintain freshness in inland Central Valley vineyards.

central-valley.name: Pacific influence
central-valley.description: Pacific air tempers Chile's warm inland valleys, especially in more western zones.

casablanca.name: Pacific fog
casablanca.description: Cold Pacific fog and breezes make Casablanca a distinctly cooler coastal region.

colchagua.name: Andes cooling
colchagua.description: Andean air and altitude influence help preserve balance in warmer parts of Colchagua.

mendoza.name: Andes altitude
mendoza.description: Altitude and meltwater from the Andes are crucial to Mendoza's viticulture and day-night temperature range.

lujan-de-cuyo.name: Andes altitude
lujan-de-cuyo.description: Moderate elevation near the Andes helps balance warm sunny days with cool nights.

uco-valley.name: High-altitude Andes sites
uco-valley.description: Very high altitude is a defining moderating factor in the Uco Valley.

salta.name: Extreme altitude
salta.description: Extreme altitude cools nights and slows ripening in one of the world's highest vineyard areas.

patagonia.name: Cool southern winds
patagonia.description: Southern latitude and persistent winds help keep Patagonia markedly cooler.

stellenbosch.name: Cape sea breezes
stellenbosch.description: Ocean breezes from False Bay and nearby cold-water influence help moderate Stellenbosch.

paarl.name: Cape cooling influence
paarl.description: Paarl is warmer inland, but still receives some moderating ocean influence from the Cape.

walker-bay.name: Cold ocean influence
walker-bay.description: Walker Bay is strongly cooled by nearby ocean influence, helping Pinot Noir and Chardonnay ripen slowly.

swartland.name: Benguela cooling
swartland.description: The Benguela current helps moderate heat in parts of Swartland, though the region remains dry and warm.

barossa.name: Elevated Eden influence
barossa.description: Higher, cooler nearby sites and local elevation differences help balance warmer Barossa conditions.

mclaren-vale.name: Gulf sea breezes
mclaren-vale.description: Sea breezes from Gulf St Vincent help moderate McLaren Vale's warm climate.

adelaide-hills.name: Altitude
adelaide-hills.description: Altitude is the key moderating factor that keeps Adelaide Hills cooler and fresher.

yarra-valley.name: Ocean influence
yarra-valley.description: Ocean influence helps keep Yarra Valley cooler than many inland Australian regions.

hunter-valley.name: Pacific influence
hunter-valley.description: Pacific influence and cloud cover help temper the Hunter Valley's warm, humid climate.

margaret-river.name: Ocean influence
margaret-river.description: Margaret River is strongly moderated by surrounding ocean influence, helping a long even ripening season.

tasmania.name: Bass Strait and Southern Ocean
tasmania.description: Cool maritime influence from Bass Strait and the Southern Ocean keeps Tasmania one of Australia's coolest regions.

rutherglen.name: Long warm ripening season
rutherglen.description: Rutherglen stays warm and sunny, allowing very ripe fruit for fortified Muscat production.

marlborough.name: Cook Strait airflow
marlborough.description: Cool maritime airflow and a large diurnal range help drive Marlborough's pungent aromatic style.

hawkes-bay.name: Pacific influence
hawkes-bay.description: Pacific influence moderates Hawke's Bay, though it remains one of New Zealand's warmer regions.

central-otago.name: Mountain shelter
central-otago.description: Mountain shelter makes Central Otago drier and more continental than most of New Zealand.

wairarapa.name: Cook Strait winds
wairarapa.description: Wind and maritime influence help moderate Wairarapa and Martinborough.
